action.reasoning: Observation: Starting with empty workspace and iteration 0. Pre-installed torch, numpy, pandas available along with data in $RAB_DATA_DIR. Analysis: A simple MLP-based direct multi-horizon forecaster on normalized data will give a quick trainable baseline (follows 'train few epochs then submit_eval' rule). Using MSE loss, basic normalization from train stats, small stride for fast first run (3 epochs), and local dummy test of predict.py to validate contract before real eval. This measures iteration time and provides initial MSE. Decision: Create model.py/train.py/predict.py from scratch, run training+validation, then submit_eval on best checkpoint.
